game_name: Boogerman: A Pick and Flick Adventure

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Specialized attack for specific enemy types
Unique enemy per level requiring puzzle solution
Resource management: red=full, yellow=one hit from death

typical_relief_triggers: Sandbox credit scroll after boss (unlimited fly-fart fuel)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Alternating combat/puzzle segments within each level, with escalating challenge per level; release valve provided by post-boss sandbox playable credits

difficulty_curve.early_game: Basic platforming and combat introduction with simple enemy patterns
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increased puzzle complexity, more enemy variety, tighter timing requirements
difficulty_curve.late_game: Escalated boss difficulty, more intricate level-specific puzzle solutions
notable_spikes: Each boss encounter marks difficulty jump
Levels progress with increasing environmental challenge

notable_level_patterns.description: Each of 20+ levels contains extensive platforming/puzzle sections followed by unique boss battle. No standard boss; each level has distinct enemy requiring that level's puzzle solution.
Levels require movement up, down, left, right, and through distant areas (non-linear vertical/horizontal progression)
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Comparison made to Disney's Aladdin in reviews

notable_level_patterns.description: After defeating boss, player enters 'sandbox' playable credit scroll with unlimited fly-fart fuel (unique resource availability vs normal gameplay)
Scattered huts throughout levels act as mid-level checkpoints when touched, reducing punishment for failure
